title: selenicereus miravallensis
intro: Selenicereus miravallensis, also known as the Miravalle cactus, is a species of flowering cactus native to Mexico. It is a type of epiphytic cactus, which grows on trees, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, pain relief, and wound healing. In herbal preparations, Selenicereus miravallensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
